steps.prompt: Which words are the finite verbs?

hints.renuntiat only: 'Possidet' is also a finite verb in the relative clause introduced by 'quae'
hints.possidet only: 'Renuntiat' is also a finite verb — the main clause verb
hints.omnibus and quae: 'Omnibus' is a pronoun/adjective and 'quae' is a relative pronoun — neither is a verb

hints.to return: 'To return' is 'redire' or 'revenire' — 'renuntiare' means 'to give up'

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'renuntiet' — 'renuntiat' ends in -at (1st conj. indic.)

hints.nuntio, nuntiare, nuntiavi, nuntiatum: These are the parts of 'nuntiare' (to announce) — the simple verb
hints.numero, numerare, numeravi, numeratum: These are the parts of 'numerare' (to count)
hints.removeo, removere, removi, remotum: These are the parts of 'removere' (to remove)

hints.to be able: 'To be able' is 'posse' — 'possidet' comes from 'possidere' (to possess)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'possideat' — 'possidet' is indicative

hints.possum, posse, potui, —: These are the parts of 'posse' (to be able)
hints.sedeo, sedere, sedi, sessum: These are the parts of 'sedere' (to sit) — the root verb in 'possidere'
hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to place)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'omnia' (neut.) — 'omnibus' is dative/ablative
hints.ablative: 'Omnibus' could be ablative, but 'renuntiare' takes a dative: 'to renounce (to) all things'
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'omnium' — 'omnibus' is dative

steps.target: relative pronoun (acc. neut. pl.) introducing a relative clause
choices: relative pronoun (acc. neut. pl.) introducing a relative clause

hints.interrogative pronoun: An interrogative 'quae' would introduce a question — this is a relative clause
hints.conjunction meaning 'and': 'And' is 'et' or 'atque' — 'quae' is a relative pronoun
hints.indefinite pronoun meaning 'some things': Indefinite 'quae' would follow 'si/nisi/num/ne' — here it is clearly relative

translation: Who does not renounce all that he possesses
